ab: teto na’a yupa esvʉtʉ no yupa baito ho ataveisi no cohzona ho hucma.
ch: W:我們約明天下午見好嗎?
en: Can I make an appointment with you tomorrow afternoon?

ab: ’uatee, teto esvʉtʉ no yupa baito nenu?
ch: M:可以，我們約哪裡見面?
en: Sure, where should we meet?

ab: teto esvʉtʉ ho yone ’oyonatmopsʉ zou? tato tas’ongka.
ch: W:我們約學校好嗎?我們要錄影。
en: How about meeting at school? We need to shoot a video.

ab: ma tee eno uhne kuba, ta’u sʉ’eʉsʉ to yʉsʉ no cou.
ch: M:那去聚會所好了，我會穿族服去。
en: Then let’s go to the community center. I’ll wear folk costume.

ab: la na’no yonghu ’o yufoyonʉ ta hosa homo seolʉa no bʉvnʉ, tako totea ho tena bʉvnʉ ho i’eima a’o.
ch: W: 我們部落的櫻花盛開很漂亮。等花開的時候你來找我吧!
en: The cherry blossoms in our tribe are beautiful when they bloom. Come find me when the flowers bloom!

ab: ma tee eno mooteo no hofungahoa, ’a tee eno angu poha’o!
ch: M: 那要等到春天，太晚了吧!
en: That would have to wait until spring. It’s too late, isn’t it?

ab: ma tako eno uhtan’e no mayasvi!
ch: W: 那你戰祭的時候來吧!
en: Then come during our Mayasvi!

ab: ’uatee! os’o na’na tongsoya!
ch: M: 好啊!我很期待!
en: Alright! I’m looking forward to it!

ab: te’on’a tuocosi, mainci miko mici yuovei ta hosa ho h’oyunsou?
ch: W: 我請問你為甚麼要搬回部落生活?
en: May I ask why you decided to move back to the tribe?

ab: ta’u yuovei ho lema’cohio.
ch: M: 我要回去教書。
en: I want to return to teach at the local school.

ab: mainci o’te yone ngeesangsi ho ma’cohio?
ch: W: 為甚麼不在都市教書?
en: Why not teach in the city?

ab: la’u’so taaecingia ’e aesiiei hosa’u, yaeza ’ucea toovei ’e hosa’u.
ch: M: 因為我懷念我的故鄉，也希望能回饋部落。
en: Because I miss my hometown and I also want to give back to the tribe.

ab: ci miko na’no meemealʉ tme’si ,sia na moso ma’cohio suu?
ch: M:你很會織布，是誰教你的?
en: You’re very skilled at weaving, who taught you?

ab: ’a i’e ba’i’u, ’a mita atva’esi meemealʉ tme’si ta hosa.
ch: W:是我的奶奶，她是我們部落最會織布的人。
en: It was my grandmother, she is the most skilled weaver in our tribe.

ab: mainci miko mici aʉsvʉtʉ tme’si?
ch: M:你怎麼會想要學織布?
en: How did you develop an interest in learning weaving?

ab: ’a ’oahngʉ to h’oyunsovamza, la’u ’ucea poa ticngicngihi ’e h’oyunsova.
ch: W:織布是我們的文化，我想要讓文化延續。
en: Weaving is a part of our culture, and I want to ensure its continuity.

ab: mo na’no mafe ’e emi no ton’u eni.
ch: M:這個小米酒真好喝。
en: This millet wine is delicious.

ab: ’a o’u iachia teai!
ch: W:是我自己釀的喔!
en: I made it myself!

ab: ci miko na’no meemealʉ, mio noana’o ho isi teai?
ch: M:你真厲害，這要釀很久嗎?
en: You’re really talented. Does it take a long time to make?

ab: o’ate, tec’o cono mainsohʉ. la pe’eni ’o ton’u ho poa tungva, tac’u pei’i ho poa cuyua hocu hoyuha to apngu, poa na’a fosoa tac’u tiz’oz’a ho poa chumua hocu hoyuha to kaiyu.
ch: W:不用，大約一週就好了。把小米先搗成粉，再倒入大鍋煮熟，再倒到篩穀器具,清涼後手揉捏成液體,就可以裝到甕裡了。
en: No, it only takes about a week. The millet first pounded into powder, and then poured into a large pot to cook, and then poured into the sieve grain utensils, cooled and kneaded by hand into a liquid, can be packed into the urn.
